title: Courgette Tart with Pesto, Ricotta & Black Olives
summary: An easy courgette tart that's perfect for rustling up on a weeknight. The whole family will love this recipe.

method.description: Heat the oven to 170°C. Pit and roughly chop the olives. Thinly slice the courgettes and set aside in a bowl.

Place the puff pastry sheet on a lined baking tray and score all around the edge, 2cm from the border. Beat the egg in a small bowl. Use a pastry brush to brush some of the egg over the scored border of the tart.
Mix the ricotta with the remaining beaten egg and the pesto. Season well with salt and pepper.
Add two-thirds of the olive oil to the courgette bowl and season well with salt and pepper.

Evenly spread the ricotta mix within the border of the tart base and arrange the sliced courgettes evenly on top. Add the olives and season again with plenty of black pepper and the remaining third of olive oil.
Bake in the oven for 30 mins until the pastry is nice and crisp and faintly golden-brown. Remove from the oven and cool on a wire rack for 5 mins before slicing.

chefs.name: Claire Thomson
chefs.summary: Claire Thomson (@5oclockapron) is a chef, author of 10 cookbooks and mum of 3 daughters. Claire began cooking professionally before smartphones were invented. Her food is delicious as it is uncomplicated, she is almost always in an apron cooking breakfast, lunch or dinner. In short, there is nothing Claire can’t cook.
